prompt: Write a product description for a pair of wireless earbuds aimed at people who work out.
output:
Most earbuds quit on you exactly when you need them most, halfway through a run, slick with sweat, one bud loose and rattling around your ear canal. These were built for that moment specifically.

The ear hooks aren't an afterthought bolted onto a regular earbud shape. They're molded to sit against the bowl of your ear and stay put through burpees, sprints, and the kind of head-whipping that ends most wireless earbuds' careers early. IPX7 water resistance means a sweat-soaked shirt or a sudden downpour mid-run isn't a problem you have to think about.

Battery runs nine hours on a single charge, enough for a long training block without a midday top-up, and the case adds three more full charges for trips where an outlet isn't guaranteed. Sound is tuned slightly bass-forward, because nobody's chasing audiophile flatness on a treadmill, they're chasing a beat that keeps their legs moving.

If your last pair of earbuds died in a gym bag somewhere around month four, this is the upgrade that actually addresses why.

prompt: Write landing page copy for a project management tool aimed at small agencies.
output:
Your agency doesn't have a project management problem. It has six of them, spread across Slack threads, a shared spreadsheet nobody updates, and the one client who still emails PDFs.

We built this for agencies specifically, not as a generic tool that happens to work for agencies. Client-facing views mean you can share progress without exposing your internal mess of comments and revisions. Time tracking is built into every task instead of bolted on as a separate app you forget to open. Retainer billing, the thing every agency tool gets wrong, actually understands that a client paying for twenty hours a month isn't the same as a client paying per project.

Setup takes under twenty minutes because we've already built the templates agencies actually use: client onboarding, campaign launches, monthly reporting. You're not staring at a blank board wondering where to start.

The agencies who switch to us usually have the same story: they tried three other tools first, all of which were either too simple to handle client complexity or too complex for a six-person team to actually adopt. This one's built for the size and shape of agency work specifically, which is the whole reason it's worth trying before your next client kickoff.

prompt: Write a short promotional email announcing a 48-hour sale on a skincare line.
output:
Subject: 48 hours, then this price is gone

We don't run sales often, mostly because the margins on small-batch skincare don't love it. But end of season means we've got inventory to move, and that's your gain for the next two days.

Everything in the line is 25% off through Wednesday at midnight, no code needed, it's already applied at checkout. That includes the vitamin C serum that's been sold out twice this year and the moisturizer people keep asking us to never discontinue.

If you've been waiting to try something or restocking your usual order, this is the window. We won't be sending another reminder after this one, and we don't do extensions.

Shop the sale before Wednesday.

topic: instagram ad for a coffee subscription
prompt: Write short ad copy for an Instagram post promoting a coffee subscription service.
output:
Your coffee shop knows your order better than some of your friends do. Time to get that same treatment at home.

We roast in small batches and ship within 48 hours of roasting, not weeks after like the bags sitting on grocery shelves. Tell us how you brew, pour-over, espresso, French press, whatever, and we send beans ground and dialed in for that specific method.

First bag's on us. Cancel anytime, no contracts, no "are you sure you want to leave" guilt trips.

Link in bio. Your mornings deserve better than whatever's been sitting in that bag since March.

prompt: Write a product description for a ceramic kitchen knife set.
output:
Steel knives dull because steel is, relatively speaking, soft. Ceramic doesn't have that problem, which is the entire reason this set exists.

Made from zirconium oxide, these blades hold an edge five to ten times longer than standard steel, which means slicing through tomatoes without crushing them and breaking down a chicken without the knife dragging halfway through. You'll sharpen this set a fraction as often as you sharpen a steel one, which for most home cooks means basically never.

They're also lighter than steel by a noticeable margin, which matters more than people expect after twenty minutes of prep work. Less wrist fatigue, more control on detail cuts.

The tradeoff, and we'd rather tell you upfront than have you find out the hard way, is that ceramic is more brittle than steel. It handles a cutting board beautifully and a marble countertop badly. Treat it as the precision tool it is, not a knife to bushwhack frozen meat with, and it'll outlast most knife sets you've owned.

topic: taglines for a meal kit service
prompt: Write a few short tagline options for a meal kit delivery service.
output:
A few directions depending on the angle you want to lead with:

On convenience: "Dinner, minus the decision fatigue."

On quality: "Restaurant ingredients. Your kitchen. Forty minutes."

On simplicity: "We pick the recipe. You get the credit."

On time: "Cook less. Eat better. Skip the grocery run."

The convenience angle tends to convert best for people who already cook but are tired of planning, while the quality angle works better for people who currently order takeout and need a reason to believe home cooking can compete.

prompt: Write short landing page copy for a language learning app.
output:
Most language apps teach you to pass their own app. You'll ace the lesson and freeze the moment an actual person asks you a real question.

This one is built around conversations you'll actually have: ordering food, asking for directions, small talk with a coworker who speaks the language natively. No grammar drills divorced from real sentences, no vocabulary lists for words you'll never say out loud.

Each lesson ends with a short voice conversation against an AI that responds the way a real person would, unpredictably, not from a script you've memorized. That's the part that actually builds the reflex you need when someone talks back to you faster than expected.

Fifteen minutes a day, six weeks in, and you'll notice the difference the next time you're somewhere that language is actually spoken, not just the next time you open the app.

topic: cold outreach email for a freelance service
prompt: Write a short cold outreach email from a freelance web designer reaching out to a local business.
output:
Subject: Quick thought on your website

Hi [Name],

I was looking for a [business type] in the area and came across yours. The food/service looks great, but I noticed the site's a bit slow to load on mobile and the menu's a little buried, which probably costs you a few customers who give up before finding what they need.

I design websites for local businesses like yours, and I'd be happy to send over a quick mockup of what an updated version could look like, no charge, no obligation. If you like it, we can talk. If not, no hard feelings.

Would a five-minute call sometime this week work to talk through what you're looking for?

Best,
[Name]

prompt: Write a product description for a weighted blanket.
output:
The science behind weighted blankets isn't mysterious or new. Deep pressure stimulation, the same principle behind a firm hug or a swaddle, triggers a measurable drop in cortisol and a rise in serotonin. This blanket just applies that pressure evenly across your whole body for eight hours instead of thirty seconds.

Glass beads sewn into evenly spaced pockets distribute weight so you're not dealing with lumps shifting to one corner by 2am. Available from 12 to 25 pounds, with the general rule being roughly 10% of your body weight, though plenty of people prefer slightly heavier for the deeper-pressure feeling.

The cover is breathable cotton, which matters more than most weighted blanket buyers expect on their first purchase. A heavy blanket that also traps heat is a blanket you stop using by July.

If you've tried melatonin, sleep apps, and a fan pointed directly at your face with limited success, this is a different mechanism entirely, one that works on your nervous system rather than your sleep schedule.

topic: twitter announcement for a feature launch
prompt: Write a short social media post announcing a new feature for a budgeting app.
output:
We just shipped the thing people have been asking for since launch: shared budgets.

Split rent, groceries, trips, whatever, with a partner or roommate without exporting spreadsheets at each other. Both of you see the same numbers in real time, no more "wait, did you already pay that?"

Update the app and look for the new Shared tab. Free on all plans, because money stuff shouldn't be a paywall.
